 {"id":"bd-1w7","title":"Feature: Backup integrity verification (asb verify)","description":"Add a verification command to check backup integrity.

## Proposed Command
```bash
asb verify [agent...]        # Verify backup integrity
asb verify --all             # Verify all backups
asb verify --repair          # Attempt to repair issues
```

## Verification Checks
1. Git repository integrity (git fsck)
2. .gitignore present and valid
3. At least one commit exists
4. HEAD is valid
5. No loose objects corruption
6. Compare backup against source (optional)

## Output
- Summary of verification status for each agent
- Warnings for potential issues
- Exit code for scripting (0 = all good, 1 = issues found)

## Use Cases
- Pre-export verification
- Post-import verification  
- Periodic health checks
- Debug backup issues

 {"id":"bd-1zq","title":"Completion: Update install.sh post-install message","description":"# Update install.sh Post-Install Message

## Task

Update the install.sh success message to mention shell completion.

## Implementation

In the verification section at the end of main():

```bash
if [[ -x "$installed_path" ]]; then
    log_success "Installation complete!"
    printf '\n' >&2

    log_info "Get started with:"
    log_info "  asb init            Initialize backup location"
    log_info "  asb backup          Backup all detected agents"
    log_info "  asb list            Show backup status"
    log_info "  asb help            Show all commands"
    
    printf '\n' >&2
    log_info "Enable tab completion:"
    log_info "  Bash: eval \"\$(asb completion bash)\""
    log_info "  Zsh:  eval \"\$(asb completion zsh)\""
    log_info "  Fish: asb completion fish | source"

    printf '\n' >&2
    log_info "Documentation: https://github.com/$REPO_OWNER/$REPO_NAME"
else
    log_error "Installation may have failed"
    exit 1
fi
```

## Why This Matters

Most users don't read documentation. Showing completion setup right after install
increases the chance they'll enable it, improving their experience.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:17.751322208Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:03:38.464083117Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:03:38.463942352Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zq","depends_on_id":"bd-39b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:17.768219405Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zq","depends_on_id":"bd-6ic","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:29:18.507250035Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-20o","title":"Stats: Implement stats_agent function","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:12:19.856317401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:51:54.957595555Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:51:54.957532878Z","close_reason":"stats_agent() implemented","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20o","depends_on_id":"bd-3jc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:12:28.809764916Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-20o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION:
Create stats_agent() function to gather backup statistics.

stats_agent() {
    local agent="$1"
    local dest=$(get_agent_backup_dir "$agent")
    
    if [[ ! -d "$dest/.git" ]]; then
        log_warn "No backup found for $agent"
        return 1
    fi
    
    local commit_count=$(git -C "$dest" rev-list --count HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    local first_commit=$(git -C "$dest" log --reverse --format="%ci" | head -1)
    local last_commit=$(git -C "$dest" log -1 --format="%ci" 2>/dev/null)
    local storage_size=$(du -sh "$dest" 2>/dev/null | cut -f1)
    local file_count=$(git -C "$dest" ls-files | wc -l)
    
    if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" == "true" ]]; then
        json_output "stats" "{...}"
    else
        # Pretty print stats
        printf "%-20s %s\n" "Total backups:" "$commit_count"
        printf "%-20s %s\n" "First backup:" "$first_commit"
        printf "%-20s %s\n" "Last backup:" "$last_commit"
        printf "%-20s %s\n" "Storage used:" "$storage_size"
        printf "%-20s %s\n" "Files tracked:" "$file_count"
    fi
}","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:14:17Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3jr","title":"Tags: Implement tag_backup function","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:09:39.212266622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:31:10.041310434Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:31:10.041262694Z","close_reason":"Implemented tag_backup() function","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jr","depends_on_id":"bd-3ag","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:09:46.537792273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-3jr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION:
Create tag_backup() function that wraps git tag.

tag_backup() {
    local agent="$1"
    local tagname="$2"
    local commit="${3:-HEAD}"
    local dest=$(get_agent_backup_dir "$agent")
    
    if [[ ! -d "$dest/.git" ]]; then
        log_error "No backup found for $agent"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate tag name (no spaces, special chars)
    if [[ ! "$tagname" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ ]]; then
        log_error "Invalid tag name. Use only letters, numbers, underscore, hyphen"
        return 1
    fi
    
    git -C "$dest" tag "$tagname" "$commit"
    log_success "Tagged $agent backup as '$tagname'"
}

Register in main() as: tag|t) tag_command "$@"","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:13:50Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3jx","title":"Export: Implement import_backup function","description":"# Implement import_backup Function

## Task

Create the import_backup() function that restores an archive.

## Implementation

```bash
import_backup() {
    local archive="$1"
    local backup_root
    backup_root=$(get_backup_root)
    
    # Validate archive
    if [[ "$archive" != "-" ]]; then
        if [[ ! -f "$archive" ]]; then
            log_error "Archive not found: ${archive}"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    
    # Dry-run mode
    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "[DRY RUN] Would import from ${archive}"
        log_info "[DRY RUN] Destination: ${backup_root}"
        
        if [[ "$archive" != "-" ]]; then
            # Show archive contents
            log_info "[DRY RUN] Archive contents:"
            tar -tzf "$archive" 2>/dev/null | head -10
        fi
        return 0
    fi
    
    log_step "Importing backup..."
    
    # Create backup location if needed
    mkdir -p "$backup_root"
    
    # Peek at archive to see what agent it contains
    local agent_folder=""
    if [[ "$archive" != "-" ]]; then
        agent_folder=$(tar -tzf "$archive" 2>/dev/null | head -1 | cut -d'/' -f1)
    fi
    
    # Check for existing backup (conflict detection)
    if [[ -n "$agent_folder" ]] && [[ -d "$backup_root/$agent_folder" ]]; then
        if [[ "$FORCE" != "true" ]]; then
            log_warn "Backup already exists: $backup_root/$agent_folder"
            
            if [[ -t 0 ]]; then
                printf 'Overwrite existing backup? [y/N] '
                local response
                IFS= read -r response
                case "$response" in
                    [yY]|[yY][eE][sS])
                        log_info "Will overwrite existing backup"
                        ;;
                    *)
                        log_info "Import cancelled"
                        return 1
                        ;;
                esac
            else
                log_error "Use --force to overwrite existing backup"
                return 1
            fi
        fi
        
        # Remove existing before extract
        rm -rf "$backup_root/$agent_folder"
    fi
    
    # Extract archive
    if [[ "$archive" == "-" ]]; then
        tar -xzf - -C "$backup_root" 2>/dev/null
    else
        tar -xzf "$archive" -C "$backup_root" 2>/dev/null
    fi
    
    if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract archive"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_success "Imported backup"
    log_info "Destination: ${backup_root}"
    log_info "Run 'asb list' to see imported backups"
    
    return 0
}
```

## Conflict Handling

If the agent already has a backup:
1. Warn user
2. In interactive mode: prompt for confirmation
3. In non-interactive mode: require --force
4. Never silently overwrite

## Stdin Mode

When archive is "-", tar reads from stdin:
- Enables piping: `ssh remote "asb export claude -" | asb import -`
- Cannot peek at contents for conflict detection
- Trust that user knows what they're doing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:25:57.331083481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:29:03.017575877Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:29:03.017427147Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jx","depends_on_id":"bd-v3p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:25:57.342613478Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-v3p","title":"Feature: Import/Export Archives","description":"# Feature: Import/Export Archives

## Problem Statement

Backups that can't be moved are only half useful. Real-world scenarios where portability matters:

1. **New machine setup**: User gets a new laptop, wants to bring agent configs
2. **Sharing configs**: Team wants to share a known-good Claude setup
3. **Disaster recovery**: User's home directory is corrupted, but they emailed an archive
4. **Cross-platform**: User switches from Linux to Mac
5. **Backup redundancy**: User wants to store backups in cloud storage

Without export/import, users must manually tar directories, figure out paths, and hope for the best.

## Solution

Add two new commands:
- `asb export <agent> [file]` - Creates portable tar.gz archive with full git history
- `asb import <file>` - Restores archive to backup location

## Technical Design

### Export Command

```bash
asb export claude                    # Creates claude-backup-20240115.tar.gz
asb export claude my-backup.tar.gz   # Creates my-backup.tar.gz
asb export claude -                  # Writes to stdout (for piping)
```

### Import Command

```bash
asb import my-backup.tar.gz          # Imports to backup location
asb import -                         # Reads from stdin (for piping)
```

### Archive Format

The archive is a standard tar.gz containing:
```
.claude/
├── .git/           # Full git history preserved!
├── .gitignore
├── settings.json
└── ...
```

The directory name tells us which agent it is.

### Implementation

**export_backup()**
```bash
export_backup() {
    local agent="$1"
    local output="${2:-}"
    local agent_name="${AGENT_NAMES[$agent]:-$agent}"
    local source
    source=$(get_agent_backup_dir "$agent")
    
    if [[ ! -d "$source/.git" ]]; then
        log_error "No backup found for ${agent_name}"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Generate default filename if not specified
    if [[ -z "$output" ]]; then
        output="${agent}-backup-$(date +%Y%m%d).tar.gz"
    fi
    
    log_step "Exporting ${agent_name} to ${output}..."
    
    # Create archive preserving git history
    local backup_root
    backup_root=$(get_backup_root)
    local folder="${AGENT_FOLDERS[$agent]}"
    
    if [[ "$output" == "-" ]]; then
        # Write to stdout
        tar -czf - -C "$backup_root" "$folder"
    else
        tar -czf "$output" -C "$backup_root" "$folder"
        
        local size
        size=$(du -h "$output" | cut -f1)
        log_success "Exported ${agent_name} (${size})"
        log_info "File: $(realpath "$output")"
    fi
}
```

**import_backup()**
```bash
import_backup() {
    local archive="$1"
    local backup_root
    backup_root=$(get_backup_root)
    
    if [[ "$archive" != "-" ]] && [[ ! -f "$archive" ]]; then
        log_error "Archive not found: ${archive}"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_step "Importing from ${archive}..."
    
    # Create backup location if needed
    mkdir -p "$backup_root"
    
    # Extract archive
    if [[ "$archive" == "-" ]]; then
        tar -xzf - -C "$backup_root"
    else
        tar -xzf "$archive" -C "$backup_root"
    fi
    
    log_success "Imported backup to ${backup_root}"
    log_info "Run 'asb list' to see imported backups"
}
```

## Important Considerations

### Git History Preservation

The entire .git directory is included in the archive. This means:
- Full backup history is preserved
- Can be used immediately after import
- No need to re-initialize or lose history

### Conflict Handling

If the agent already has a backup at the destination:
- Show warning
- Require --force to overwrite
- Or prompt for confirmation in interactive mode

### Stdin/Stdout Support

The "-" argument enables piping:
```bash
# Remote transfer
asb export claude - | ssh remote "asb import -"

# S3 backup
asb export claude - | aws s3 cp - s3://bucket/claude-backup.tar.gz

# Encryption
asb export claude - | gpg -c > claude-backup.tar.gz.gpg
```

## Testing Checklist

- [ ] Export creates valid tar.gz
- [ ] Export includes .git directory
- [ ] Import extracts to correct location
- [ ] Imported backup works with asb history
- [ ] Default filename includes date
- [ ] Custom filename works
- [ ] Stdout/stdin piping works
- [ ] Dry-run shows what would be exported/imported
- [ ] Force flag handles conflicts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:25:14.535112113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:25:47.494362932Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:25:47.494272021Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-v3p","depends_on_id":"bd-3u9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:25:14.550127646Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-yug","title":"Unit Test: create_unit_test_harness","description":"Create the harness that sources asb without running main().

## Implementation
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# tests/unit/harness.sh
# Source asb script without executing

# Prevent main() from running
ASB_SOURCED=true

# Source the script
SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/asb"

# Now all functions are available for testing
```

## Modifications to asb
Add at the end of asb script:
```bash
if [[ "${ASB_SOURCED:-false}" \!= "true" ]]; then
    main "$@"
fi

 {"id":"bd-zh2","title":"Feature: Auto-Discovery of New Agents (asb discover)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:09:12.291973937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:06:31.357893721Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:06:31.357829090Z","close_reason":"Implemented discover command with --auto, --list, --json support and shell completions","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-zh2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PURPOSE: Scan for common AI agent config patterns and suggest new agents to add.

WHY THIS MATTERS:
- New users do not need to know every agent config location
- Purely additive - does not change existing behavior
- AI tooling evolves rapidly; helps users stay current
- Lower barrier to entry

COMMAND:
- asb discover - Scan and interactively prompt to add
- asb discover --auto - Automatically add all found agents
- asb discover --list - Just list found agents without prompting
- asb discover --json - JSON output of discovered agents

DISCOVERY PATTERNS (internal database):
Known patterns to scan for:
~/.kodu           -> Kodu
~/.continue       -> Continue
~/.sourcegraph    -> Sourcegraph CLI
~/.tabby          -> Tabby
~/.cody           -> Cody
~/.copilot        -> GitHub Copilot CLI
~/.tabnine        -> Tabnine
~/.supermaven     -> Supermaven
~/.aide           -> Aide
~/.roo            -> Roo Code
~/.void           -> Void Editor

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Create DISCOVERY_PATTERNS associative array
- Check each pattern against existing AGENT_FOLDERS
- Interactive mode: prompt y/N for each found
- Store custom agents in config file
- Add get_custom_agents() to load user additions
- Merge AGENT_FOLDERS with custom agents at startup

OUTPUT FORMAT:
Scanning for AI coding agents...

Found 2 new agents:

  ~/.kodu         Kodu (AI coding assistant)
  ~/.continue     Continue (open-source copilot)

Add these agents? [y/N]

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Discovers agents not already in AGENT_FOLDERS
- Interactive prompts work correctly
- --auto mode adds without prompting
- Custom agents persist in config
- Newly added agents work with backup/restore
- Tests cover discovery and addition","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:09:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zkw","title":"Documentation: Update README.md with all new features","description":"# Update README.md with All New Features

## Task

Comprehensive update to README.md documenting all v0.2 features.

## Sections to Add/Update

### New Features Section
```markdown
## New in v0.2

- **Dry-run mode**: Preview changes with `--dry-run` or `-n`
- **Restore confirmation**: See exactly what will change before restoring
- **Configuration file**: Persistent settings in `~/.config/asb/config`
- **Export/Import**: Portable archives with `asb export` and `asb import`
- **Shell completion**: Tab completion for bash, zsh, and fish
```

### Update Installation Section
Add shell completion setup instructions.

### Update Usage Section
- Add global flags (--dry-run, --force, --verbose)
- Add new commands (config, export, import, completion)
- Update existing command descriptions

### Add Configuration Section
```markdown
## Configuration

asb can be configured via config file or environment variables:

### Config File
```bash
asb config init    # Create config file
asb config show    # View current settings
```

Config file location: `~/.config/asb/config`

### Environment Variables
| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| ASB_BACKUP_ROOT | ~/.agent_settings_backups | Backup location |
| ASB_AUTO_COMMIT | true | Auto-commit on backup |
| ASB_VERBOSE | false | Verbose output |
```

### Add Portability Section
```markdown
## Portability

Export backups for transfer between machines:
```bash
asb export claude                    # Create archive
asb import claude-backup.tar.gz     # Import on new machine
```

Pipe support for remote transfer:
```bash
asb export claude - | ssh remote "asb import -"
```
```

## Dependencies

This task depends on ALL feature implementations being complete,
